entry.title: Re: Get Application Job Status of a Periodic Job
entry.content:
Yes. The job name and count only gives me the status of the first job occurrence. But what i need is the succeeding job occurrence’ status.

If the first job occurrence is done, job status shows as finished when checked using job name count. It doesnt give the status of the next job occurrence.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Output Management vs. NAST: When to Choose Which?
entry.content: I think most of the cons mentioned are already addressed in OPD version (form flexibility in Adobe Forms, CDS View extensibility, support for Sales Document). Looks more scalable and flexible in newer version. Although not all SAP applications are on board as of 2022 yet. Maybe still in the works. Anyone has a road map?

entry.title: Re: FAQ Solution Types and Charging Model
entry.content:
Hi.

  I read a lot of things and watched some you tube videos to understand SAP CAL work scheme. I'm not native English speaker so it's a little bit hard to understand it. Can someone answer me that I got it right or no? My conclusion: I want to learn some SAP module. There is TRIAL SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully Activated Appliance currently. I calculated cost it's 4.94$/H and 203.34$/M. I can get 30 day free Appliance license but I still need to pay for instance by this three statuses: Active, Suspended and Terminated. 
   I'll be so grateful if you explain me with easiest way. There are so my new terms when I read something about deploying SAP to Google Cloud Service. Or maybe I can be able to use EDU type of SAP? And I want to know that calculated cost is common with Google Cloud server payment or not(if II use GCP as provider). 

Regards,
Abdulaziz

entry.title: Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content:
HI @PierreB I am getting the below error while running the cmd. for downloading libraries 

ng add @spartacus/schematics@2211.29 --no-interactive

note :  tried latest version and other versions as well still same error

error:Using package manager: npm
× Unable to fetch package information for '@spartacus/schematics@2211.29': 403 Forbidden - GET https://73554900100900004337.npmsrv.base.repositories.cloud.sap/@spartacus%2fschematics , I didnt find any relevant solution yet

any quick resolution appreciated please.

entry.title: SAP AIF persistence for Custom WS Synchronous messages
entry.content:
Hello,

We are in a migration journey from PO to CPI, we are planning to move our custom ABAP XI Proxies towards WS in SOAMANAGER. One of the monitoring drawback in this approach is the persistence of Synchronous messages are not displayed like we used to see in SXI_MONITOR. We are still in ECC on backend though.

Is there any possibility to adopt custom solution to have the persistence of WS Synchronous messages in AIF monitoring or in SRT_MONI for custom WS Synchronous messages ?  As of now only Error messages are logged into SRT_ELOG.

Thanks.

entry.title: Sales Order Data transfer from sap to outer external Server
entry.content:
API

As a newbie ABAP developer, I need to transfer sales order (standard fields) data from SAP to an external server.

I'm required to use a POST API method for this data transfer.

Could you please guide me on the necessary steps or provide me with resources on how to achieve this?

entry.title: Resolving SAP IDOC Errors: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Understanding the Error 🧐

Before diving into solutions, it's crucial to understand the specific error message associated with the IDOC. This will provide valuable clues about the root cause. Common error types include:

* Application errors: Issues within the target system.
* Syntax errors: Problems with the IDOC structure or data.
* System errors: Technical problems with the SAP system.

Common Steps to Resolve IDOC Errors ⛔️

* Identify the Error:
* Use transaction codes like WE02, WE05, or BD87 to view the IDOC details and error message.
* Analyze the error message carefully to understand the problem.
* Correct Data Issues:
* If the error is due to incorrect data, rectify the data in the source system.
* Ensure data consistency and completeness.
* Check IDOC Structure:
* Verify the IDOC structure (message type, segments, fields) matches the target system's expectations.
* Use transaction WE30 to check the IDOC structure.
* Review Partner Profiles:
* Check the partner profile for the correct address, communication methods, and authentication details.
* Use transaction WE20 to manage partner profiles.
* Debug the IDOC Processing:
* Use transaction SM50 to analyze the ABAP program handling the IDOC.
* Set breakpoints to understand the program's logic and identify the error point.
* Reprocess the IDOC:
* Once the issue is resolved, use transaction BD87 to reprocess the IDOC.
* Consider using the "Correct and Process" option if applicable.
* Check for Custom Enhancements:
* If custom code is involved, examine it for potential errors.
* Debug the custom code to identify issues.
* Monitor IDOC Status:
* Use transaction SM58 to monitor IDOC status and retry failed transmissions.
* Check for network or system-related issues.
* Consult Documentation:
* Refer to SAP documentation for specific error messages and resolution steps.
* Search for relevant notes on the SAP Support Portal.

Additional Tips: 🛜

* Test Thoroughly: Make changes in a test environment before deploying to production.
* Involve Experts: Seek help from SAP consultants or experienced ABAP developers if needed.
* Error Handling: Implement proper error handling mechanisms in your IDOC processes.
* Logging: Enable detailed logging to track IDOC processing and troubleshoot issues.

Need More Specific Help? 👍

If you can provide more details about the error message, IDOC type, and system environment, I can offer more tailored guidance.
Would you like to share more information about the IDOC error you're facing? How to get a list of Idocs in Error?

SAP BD64 is a transaction code used in SAP to manage distribution models, which define how data is distributed between different systems or applications. Distribution models are often implemented using IDocs (Inter-document), a standard data format for exchanging data within and between SAP systems.

WE21 is a transaction code in SAP used to manage ports for IDocs. An IDoc (Inter-document) is a standard data format used by SAP to exchange data between different systems or applications. A port is a logical connection point that defines how an IDoc is sent or received.

Please feel free to ask any further questions.

entry.title: Re: Resolving SAP IDOC Errors: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_resolving-idoc-errors-a-step-by-step-guide-activity-7231570352345092096-J43X?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: Understanding SAP IDocs
entry.content:
SAP IDocs (Intermediate Documents) are a standardized format for data exchange between SAP systems and external systems. They are used to transfer data in a structured and reliable way.

Commonly Used SAP IDoc Types
While there are numerous IDoc types, here are some of the most frequently used:
* ORDERS: Used for purchase orders.
* INVOIC: Used for invoices.
* DESADV: Used for delivery schedules.
* MATMAS: Used for material master data.

Key Points to Remember:
* IDoc Structure: IDocs have a specific structure, including segments and fields, which defines the data to be transferred.
* IDoc Types: Different IDoc types have different structures tailored to specific business processes.
* IDoc Processing: IDocs are processed through various steps, including inbound processing, application processing, and outbound processing.
* ALE (Application Link Enabling): ALE is the framework used to exchange IDocs between SAP systems.
* EDI (Electronic Data Interchange): IDocs can be used for EDI, allowing data exchange with external systems in standard formats.

SAP IDoc: 50 Transaction Codes You Need To Know
Find below a list of the SAP IDoc & EDI related transaction codes which I personally find useful.

SAP IDoc: 50 Transaction Codes You Need To Know
Find below a list of the SAP IDoc & EDI related transaction codes which I personally find useful.


BD51 Maintain function modules (inbound)
BD55 Maintain IDoc Conversion
BD62 Define Segment Conversion Rule
BD79 Maintain IDoc Conversion Rules
BD87 Status Monitor for ALE Messages
BDM2 Monitoring: IDocs in Receiving Sys.
BDM7 ALE Audit: statistical analyses
IDOC IDoc: Repair and check programs
SM58 Asynchronous RFC Error Log
SM59 RFC Destinations (Display/Maintain)
WE02 Display IDoc
WE05 IDoc Lists
WE06 Active IDoc monitoring
WE07 IDoc statistics
WE09 Search for IDocs by Content
WE10 Search for IDocs by Content
WE11 Delete IDocs
WE12 Test Modified Inbound File
WE14 Test Outbound Processing
WE15 Test Outbound Processing from MC
WE16 Test Inbound File
WE17 Test Status File
WE18 Create Status File
WE19 Test tool
WE20 Partner Profiles
WE21 Port definition
WE24 DefaultValuesForOutboundParameters
WE27 DefaultValues for Inbound Parameters
WE30 IDoc Type Development
WE31 Development IDoc Segment
WE32 Development IDoc View
WE34 Object for Display of XML IDocs
WE41 Process codes, outbound
WE42 Process codes, inbound
WE43 Funct.module: Status record display
WE44 Partner Types and Checks
WE45 Forward (inbound) (V3, EDILOGADR)
WE47 Status Maintenance
WE55 Function Module for Path Names
WE57 Assignment Messages for Appl. Objs
WE60 Documentation for IDoc types
WE64 Documentation message types
WE70 Conversion: Basic types
WE71 Conversion: Extensions
WE72 Conversion: IDoc types
WE73 Conversion: Logical messages
WE81 Logical message types
WE82 Assign Messages for IDoc Type
WELI Define Status Groups
WLF_IDOC IDoc Processing

entry.title: Re: Understanding SAP IDocs
entry.content: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_understanding-sap-idocs-sap-idocs-intermediate-activity-7259454475600367618-dlcN?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: IDoc Failure and Resolution in SAP
entry.content:
IDocs (Intermediate Documents) in SAP are used for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) to exchange data between SAP and external systems. However, IDoc processing can encounter errors resulting in failures. Here's a breakdown of IDoc failures and their resolution process, along with an example:

**IDoc Failure:**

An IDoc failure occurs when there's an issue during data exchange, preventing successful transfer of information. This can happen due to various reasons:

* **Data Mapping Errors:** Incorrect mapping between SAP data fields and the external system's format.
* **Missing or Invalid Data:** Required data fields missing in the IDoc or containing invalid values.
* **Communication Issues:** Network connectivity problems between SAP and the external system.
* **Application Errors:** Errors in the receiving application logic causing processing issues.

**Resolving IDoc Failures:**

Here's a general process for resolving IDoc failures:

1. **Identify the Error:** Use transaction WE05 (Display IDoc) to check the IDoc status and error message. The message provides details about the cause of the failure.
2. **Analyze the Error:** Based on the error message, pinpoint the root cause (data mapping, missing data, etc.).
3. **Resolve the Issue:**
* Fix data mapping errors in the configuration.
* Correct missing or invalid data in the originating document.
* Troubleshoot network connectivity issues.
* Work with the application team to address application-specific errors.
4. **Reprocess the IDoc:** Once the issue is resolved, use transaction WE19 (Reprocess IDoc) to attempt processing the IDoc again.

**Example:**

An IDoc for a sales order fails with an error message indicating a missing customer number. The resolution steps might involve:

* Checking the originating sales order in transaction VA01.
* Verifying if the customer number is missing or incorrect.
* Updating the customer number in the sales order.
* Reprocessing the IDoc using WE19.

**Additional Tips:**

* Use transaction SM37 (Job Overview) to monitor background jobs related to IDoc processing.
* Leverage table EDIDS to track IDoc statuses and detailed error logs.
* For complex errors, consider involving SAP Basis or EDI specialists for further assistance.

By following these steps and understanding the potential causes of IDoc failures, you can effectively troubleshoot and resolve issues to ensure smooth data exchange between SAP and external systems.

entry.title: Re: IDoc Failure and Resolution in SAP
entry.content: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_idoc-failure-and-resolution-in-sap-idocs-activity-7189668688751415298-8c1s?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: Transfer of payment card details from S/4 Service Order to DMR/CMR [Resource Related Billing - DP92]
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA, an SCP app named “Payment Hub” introduced for digital payment card management. Payment Hub provides the interaction between SAP applications and payment service providers. SAP applications can use the payment hub to register, authorize, settle the payment cards by payment service providers, without saving sensitive data in the SAP systems.   

And “ in S4 HANA Service, the SAP digital payments add-on is integrated with service order processing. And it supports the SAP digital payments add-on for processing credit card payments.” 

During service processes, credit cards for one-time use are registered through the Payment Hub by a service provider. Once registered, a token is issued to the SAP system for operations such as authorization and settlement. 

  • If service processes use BDR (Billing Document Request) to complete their billing process, this token will be transferred to SAP FI.
  • In case of service sales integration, token is transferred to sales items via service order integration to sales order process. 
  • In S/4HANA Service, service confirmations generate Billing Request Documents (BDRs), which are used to create billing documents. In such use cases, payment card data and authorizations from service confirmations are copied into BDRs and billing documents. 
  • SAP systems store only card tokens, showing limited details like the masked card number and cardholder name. In service processes, cards are authorized automatically for billing-relevant items. Settlement and advice are managed by SAP FI. 

After billing document creation: 

  • Authorization status is set to “Used,” and the billing document ID is saved in the authorization data. 
  • Authorizations cannot be updated anymore. 
  • If a billing document is cancelled, the authorization status reverts to “Open”, and the Billing Document ID is removed.  

For more detailed information about the integration of SAP digital payments add-on with service order processing. Please refer the link.

This section of the blog explains how payment card details are transferred from the advance execution order items summarized [F] or Itemized [G] billing relevance to the DMR/CMR document during resource related billing process [Transaction DP92]. 

During resource related billing process, payment cards registered via service orders are replicated to Debit/Credit Memo Request (DMR/CMR). The DMR/CMR handles authorization for the open amount and generates new authorizations for partial billings based on existing SD logic. Further the authorizations in the DMR/CMR are maintained independently and are not returned to the service order. 

Points to be remember:  

  • Payment card details cannot be deleted from the service order if linked to any DMR/CMR. 
  • Payment card integration feature is currently supported for payer maintained on service order header level. 

     Integration of payment card with AEO item with summarized or itemized billing relevance.

Prerequisites: 

  1. Connection to Payment Hub 
    • Payment Hub is an Add-on product of S4/HANA. In the SAP Note 2524512, the details for connecting payment hub are described, like RFC connection, creation of card type, activation of payment hub, and activation of scenarios.  
    • Payment Scenario 05 will be used for AEO items (Billing Rel. Summarized/Itemized). Through scenario ‘05’, the usage of payment hub is activated additionally for SD sales order. If scenario is not activated, the ‘Payment Method’ assignment block will not appear in service transactions. 

     2. Activate Payment Card for Service Transaction 

  • Create checking group for payment cards. 

Customer management uses the same checking group for payment card as Sales and Distribution.  

SPRO: Sales and Distribution->Billing->Payment Cards->Authorization and Settlement->Maintain Checking Groups 

  • Assign payment plan type and checking group to service transaction type.  

 Payment plan type ‘03’ is defined system widely for payment cards.  

 SPRO: Customer Management->Transactions->Basic Settings->Define Transaction Types (under the transaction category ‘Sales’ the assignment can be done.) 

Process Flow 

The following steps outline the process flow for transferring payment card details from service order (AEO item (Billing Rel. Summarized/Itemized)) to the DMR/CMR document during the resource-related billing process via DP92: 

1. Create a Service Order having an execution item. 

 2. Navigate to the item overview page. Scroll to the Billing assignment block, change the Billing Relevance to "Summarized Billing," and assign a DIP profile. 

3. Navigate to the Payment Card assignment block on the service order page while in edit mode. Use the "Register Payment Card" button to register a payment card. 

Note: To use a card linked to the payer, select "Add Existing Card."  

4.  Enter the card details and click on submit. 

After successful submission, a dialog box will display the message: “Your request has been sent to the service provider. The screen will be closed now.” 

Click on Ok button and close the pop-up. You can see payment card has been added to “Payment Cards” assignment block.

5.  Release the advance execution item. 

Note:  After releasing the AEO item with Billing Relevance set to "Summarized," you can verify that no authorization has been generated under payment card section, as expected.

Also, in the “Transaction History” section, verify the generation of the Plant Maintenance Order (PMO)

6. Launch “Change Maintenance Order” application through launchpad and release the Plant Maintenance Order (PMO). Then save it. 

7. Launch “Enter PM Order Confirmation” application and create confirmation corresponding to Plant Maintenance Order. 

8. Then the next step is to Create Debit Memo Request through app “Create Resource-Related Billing Request – Service Order” [DP92].

 Enter Service Order and service order item. Click on “Billing Document Request” button.       

You can verify that a DMR document has been created, containing the payment card details assigned to the Service Order, without any authorization. 

9. To create an authorization for the DMR document, ensure the document is completed and saved without any errors. Once saved successfully, the authorization will be created automatically, as demonstrated below. 

This ensures the successful transfer of payment card details from Service Order to Debit Memo Request during the resource related billing process.

entry.title: Defining Local Fields in SAP QUERY
entry.content:
Local Fields in SAP Query: Enhancing Reporting Flexibility

Local fields in SAP Query are calculated fields that you define within the query itself. They don't exist in the underlying database tables or InfoSet. This allows you to perform calculations, transformations, or logical operations on existing fields without needing to create new fields in the database. This is very useful for reporting purposes.

Key Benefits of Local Fields:

  • Flexibility: Create custom calculations and derive new information without modifying the underlying data structure.
  • Performance: Calculations are performed during query execution, avoiding the need to store redundant data.
  • Simplicity: Define complex logic directly within the query, making it easier to manage and maintain.

Example: Calculating Delivery Variance (Your Scenario)

Let's use your example of the BACKLOGVENTES InfoSet to illustrate how to define a local field for delivery variance:

Scenario: You want to see the difference between the referenced quantity (DELIVERY) and the confirmed quantity (SALES ORDER). This difference represents the delivery variance.

Steps to Define a Local Field:

  1. Open SAP Query (SQ01/SQ02): Navigate to SAP Query using transaction codes SQ01 (Queries) or SQ02 (InfoSets).

  2. Edit/Create a Query: Open the relevant query based on the BACKLOGVENTES InfoSet.

  3. Go to the "Fields" tab: This is where you select the fields to be included in your report.

  4. Define a Local Field:

    • Click on the "Local Fields" button (usually represented by an icon of a calculator or a field with a plus sign).

    • Enter a name for your local field (e.g., DELIVERY_VARIANCE).

    • Enter a description (e.g., "Difference between Referenced and Confirmed Quantity").

    • Crucially, enter the formula: This is where you define the calculation. In your case, the formula would be:

      DELIVERY - SALES_ORDER

      (Assuming DELIVERY represents the "Referenced quantity in base unit of measure" and SALES_ORDER represents the "Confirmed Quantity". Adjust the field names to match your InfoSet.)

    • Specify the data type and length of the local field, if necessary. Usually, the query tool infers this based on the formula.

  5. Add the Local Field to the Output: Select the newly created local field (DELIVERY_VARIANCE) to be included in your report output.

  6. Execute the Query: Run the query to see the results, including the calculated delivery variance.

Example in More Detail (with potential field names):

Let's say the actual field names in your InfoSet are:

  • VBAP-KWMENG (Confirmed Quantity in Sales Order - often aliased as SALES_ORDER in the Infoset)
  • LIPS-LFIMG (Delivered Quantity / Referenced Quantity in delivery - often aliased as DELIVERY)

Then, your local field definition would be:

  • Name: DELIVERY_VARIANCE
  • Description: "Difference between Delivered and Confirmed Quantity"
  • Formula: LIPS-LFIMG - VBAP-KWMENG or DELIVERY - SALES_ORDER (depending on how the fields are aliased in your InfoSet)

Important Considerations:

  • Data Types: Ensure the data types of the fields used in the formula are compatible. You might need to use type conversions (e.g., NUMC to DEC) if necessary.
  • Aliases: If your InfoSet uses aliases for field names, use the aliases in your formula.
  • Error Handling: Consider potential errors, such as dividing by zero. You can use conditional statements (e.g., IF...THEN...ELSE) to handle these cases.

By using local fields effectively, you can create powerful and flexible reports in SAP Query without needing to modify the underlying data structure, making reporting much easier and more efficient.

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF persistence for Custom WS Synchronous messages
entry.content:
Hello,

Customize Message Logging for Synchronous WS Calls
SAP provides options to customize the logging behavior for synchronous Web Service calls.

Enable Message Logging in SRT
Navigate to transaction SRT_UTIL and enable logging for synchronous messages.
In the Runtime Configuration tab, set the following for your service:
Enable Request Logging and Response Logging.
Adjust the logging levels for both Request and Response to ensure all messages are captured.
Adjust Logging Settings
Check SRT Framework Settings to ensure logging is activated for synchronous calls.
Use transaction SICF to configure the specific Web Services nodes.
2. Enhance Logging with Custom Implementation
By default, SRT_MONI and SRT_ELOG log only error messages. To include all synchronous messages:

Implement a custom handler class via BAdI: SRT_MONI_LOGGING.
In the handler, enhance the logic to persist synchronous Web Service messages based on your requirements (e.g., capture every message, not just errors).
3. Integrate with AIF for Monitoring
If you want to extend monitoring capabilities to SAP AIF:

Configure AIF interfaces for your synchronous Web Services.
Map the incoming and outgoing message structures to AIF’s interface-specific data structures.
Enhance AIF error handling to process and log synchronous Web Service calls using custom logic or framework extensions.
4. Persist and Retrieve Synchronous Messages
Use a custom persistence mechanism to store all synchronous messages (not just errors) for future analysis:

Create custom database tables for message storage.
Modify your synchronous Web Service logic to persist all request and response messages in these tables.
Write a program or use AIF utilities to read from these tables and display the logs.
5. Use SOAMANAGER Configuration
Ensure your Web Service configuration in SOAMANAGER supports detailed logging:

Enable detailed logging under the Provider System settings for the relevant service.
Test the configuration to confirm that all synchronous messages are captured.
6. Monitor Using SRT_MONI and Custom Solutions
Once implemented:

Use SRT_MONI for standard SAP Web Service monitoring.
Extend with a custom solution, integrating logs into a central monitoring dashboard in AIF or a custom-built ABAP report.
Additional Tips
Consider SOAP Runtime Tracing (transaction SRT_UTIL) during the development and testing phase to ensure proper logging of synchronous Web Service calls.
Regularly clean up log data if synchronous messages are persisted for monitoring to avoid performance issues.   
Maryland Well being Connection
Ensure compliance with data retention policies, especially if sensitive data is logged.

entry.title: Issue related to the PR creation under the asset category
entry.content:
Hi all, my question is related to the PR creation under the asset category:

  1. Created PR Against in asset category & GL code and the relevant WBS number is assigned.
  2. Now I’m checking the commitment in PR (after final approval), no commitment is showing against the PR, as well as no commitment is showing in slr558 report.

My requirement is to show this PR cost in commitment cost against the WBS as per the client need.

Please help me which setting I need to cheque in the FI and MM to achieve this.

entry.title: Data_Quality_Rules
entry.content:
Hi SAP users,

Is there a list of standard data quality rules for supply planning or other functions in SAP? Any guidance on identifying effective data quality rules would be appreciated

Thank you🙂

George A.

entry.title: Re: How to Call RAP Actions with Table Types from a List Report
entry.content:
Hi @DiegoValdivia,

I am using OAuth 2.0 with the "Password Credentials" grant type. The credentials are retrieved from the service key of the ABAP environment.

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF persistence for Custom WS Synchronous messages
entry.content:
@SimhaJK2024 wrote:

Hello,

We are in a transition from PO to CPI, we are planning to move our custom ABAP XI Proxies towards WS in SOAMANAGER. One of the monitoring drawback in this approach is the persistence of Synchronous messages like we used to see in SXI_MONITOR. We are still in ECC on backend though.

Is there any possibility to adopt custom solution to have the persistence of WS Synchronous in AIF monitoring in SRT_MONI for custom WS for every message ?  As of now only Error messages are logged into SRT_ELOG.

Thanks. 

Hello,
We're transitioning from PO to CPI and planning to move our custom ABAP XI Proxies to WS in SOAMANAGER. One issue is the persistence of synchronous messages in monitoring. Currently, only error messages are logged into SRT_ELOG. Can anyone suggest a custom solution for logging all WS synchronous messages in AIF monitoring through SRT_MONI? Join PD


Best Regards,
Daniel Lopez

entry.title: Re: Creating unit test data in the RAP context
entry.content:
@felixw 

CDS test double mocks data sources that are just one tier below the CDS view under test. In your case, as you want to test the Z_I_ views, change the data type of the mock data from the table type to the base entity type as shown below:

DATA my_mock_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF Z_R_ENTITY_A.

entry.title: Deployment Error : 403 error in Fiori App deployed in S4Hana Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP-ians,

I am currently working on an integration project between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and a External API. 
I am facing an issue with my Fiori app deployed on the S/4HANA Public Cloud. The app is working fine locally, but when deployed on the cloud, I am encountering a 403 Forbidden error when attempting to access backend data.

The solution involves the following steps:

  1. CDS and CAP Application:

    • I have created a Core Data Services (CDS) view in public cloud and consumed it in SAP BTP Business Application Studio (BAS).
    • I have developed a CAP application where I consumed the CDS OData service and implemented the backend logic for posting data to the External API.

  2. Deployment to SAP BTP:

    • The CAP backend is deployed to Cloud Foundry in SAP BTP, and I have created a destination for the deployed BAS service URL, which I am using to connect the Fiori app to the backend.
    • I have also tested my CAP backend service in Postman it is working fine.

       


      In Cap Destination I have also tried using the Authentication type as OAuth2clientCredentials.

  3. Fiori App Development:

    • I created a Fiori app that consumes the backend data from the CAP application. (the Fiori and CAPM are two different Projects in BAS).
    • The app works fine when running locally in the SAP BTP BAS environment. I'm able to interact with the external API through CAP backend as expected.

       

  4. Deployment to S/4HANA Public Cloud:

    • I successfully deployed the Fiori app as a Fiori tile to the S/4HANA Public Cloud.
    • In public Cloud, While accessing the deployed Application, the UI of the app loads correctly, I am facing a 403 error when trying to access the backend data (while loading the metadata of the backend).

       

  5. IAM and Authorization:

    • While Deployment, I have created the IAM App in ADT for the app, but since I am using a CAP backend (not RAP), I am unsure of how to bind the service of CAP to the Application to resolve the 403 error. Typically, the RAP service is bound to the app to resolve such issues, but since I am not using RAP, I need guidance on how to handle this scenario. I didn't added any services to this APP.

       

    • Additionally, I see a UAA roles is created in BTP environment up on deployment of CAPM application, if I assign the role to user will it resolve this 403 error.

       

My Questions:

  • What could be causing the 403 error when accessing the CAP backend from the deployed Fiori app on S/4HANA Public Cloud? is it due to lack of authorization or roles in BTP?
  • Are there specific configurations or steps I need to follow when using a CAP backend instead of RAP for Fiori apps deployed on S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • Is there any option available to import the CAP service to ABAP environment so that I could add the CAP service to application in IAM?
  • How can I resolve the 403 error and ensure that the Fiori app can access the backend data?
  • Will the error resolve if I assign the UAA roles to the respective User in BTP?

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Many Thanks in Advance!


Best Regards,
Arasu

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA ,SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Plants Setup
entry.content:
A company has several units in the same location also the other units in other locations, what is the best approach to create plants for the same location? Do we create a single plant with all the other units as storage location for the same location or we create individual plants for each of the units for the same location?

Regards,

Sagnik

entry.title: How can get the two last record of a internal table and pass to other table?
entry.content:
Dear experts:

How can get the two last record of a internal table and pass to other table?

I have those two options:

 

 

DATA: it_tabla_interna TYPE TABLE OF your_type, " Table original
      it_tabla_destino TYPE TABLE OF your_type,  " Table target
      lv_count TYPE i.

lv_count = lines( it_tabla_interna ).

IF lv_count >= 2.
  READ TABLE it_tabla_interna INDEX lv_count TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    APPEND it_tabla_interna[ lv_count ] TO it_tabla_destino.
  ENDIF.


  READ TABLE it_tabla_interna INDEX lv_count - 1 TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    APPEND it_tabla_interna[ lv_count - 1 ] TO it_tabla_destino.
  ENDIF.

ENDIF.

 

And I have other option using LOOP and with access Indice:

 

 

DATA: it_tabla_interna TYPE TABLE OF your_type,  " Table original
      it_tabla_destino TYPE TABLE OF your_type,  " Tabla target
      lv_count TYPE i.

lv_count = lines( it_tabla_interna ).


IF lv_count >= 2.


  APPEND it_tabla_interna[ lv_count ] TO it_tabla_destino.


  APPEND it_tabla_interna[ lv_count - 1 ] TO it_tabla_destino.

ENDIF.

 

However I want know if exist other options different?

Regards

entry.title: Re: New Information Retrieval Techniques in SAP HANA Cloud using BM25 and ANNS for Advanced Text Min
entry.content:
Concerning RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), I was aware of the 2 search methods "L2 distance" and "cosine similarity". It was already unclear to me when to use what. I understand that "BM25" and "ANNS" are 2 additional search methods and that there are even some variantes using "Vector PCA" and "hybrid search". Do you have any decision tree to decide on which method to use depending on data volume, ressource consumption, speed, expected quality of the result,...?
Other question, the "Vector Call Search" API of SAP AI Core does not have any parameter to specify the search method to be used. Is it something that is in the roadmap ? What is the method currently used?

BR,

Erkin

entry.title: Re: How do you share a postgresql instance across multipl...
entry.content:
Hi Phani,

right now I would think that you have to provide a separate instance for each application. Not like with SAP HANA Cloud that you use the same backend instance and create separate HDI containers.

But maybe check at https://github.com/cap-js/cds-dbs/issues if a corresponding issue exists and create one if not.

Best Regards
Gregor

entry.title: Re: While Running Job for full indexing . i am getting be...
entry.content: didn't work jane_zhu2

entry.title: Re: BSP: Problem displaying icon using ITERATOR
entry.content:
HI!

I found the solution to the problem.

I changed the code shown above.

            WHEN 'CONTROL'.
              ASSIGN COMPONENT p_column_key OF STRUCTURE <fs> TO <l_field>.
              if <l_field> is assigned .
                wf_text = <l_field> .
              endif .
              if wf_text eq '@0A@'.
                figure_ctrl = 'Triste.png'.
              elseif wf_text eq '@09@'.
                figure_ctrl = 'Media.png'.
              elseif wf_text eq '@08@'.
                figure_ctrl = 'Feliz.png'.
              endif.
              icon_ctrl = CL_BSP_MIMES=>SAP_ICON( id = figure_ctrl ).
              p_replacement_bee = CL_HTMLB_IMAGE=>FACTORY( src = figure_ctrl width = '20' height = '20' ).
*              p_replacement_bee = CL_HTMLB_IMAGE=>FACTORY( id = p_cell_id src = icon_ctrl ).

New Screen :

Please help me with the right link to activate right link for Identity Authentication service.

Good day, Seniors,

Candidate is unable to complete/submit "Complete Your E-Signature" task during the onboarding (2.O) process. when admin try Nudge from onboarding dashboard getting following error, please help me.

Error

Entity ONB2ProcessOverview,ONB2ActivityOverview,ONB2ActivityResponsible,getOnboardingBPEUserTaskStatus,ONB2Process,ONB2DataCollectionUserConfig,PerEmail,PicklistOption,User,PickListV2,ONB2BuddyActivity,ONB2EquipmentActivity,ONB2EquipmentType,ONB2EquipmentTypeValue,Attachment,ONB2GoalActivity,ONB2Goal,ONB2GoalCategoryConfig,ONB2LinkActivity,ONB2Link,ONB2LinkConfig,ONB2MeetingActivity,ONB2MeetingEvent,ONB2CentralMeetingEvent,ONB2MeetingParticipant,ONB2CentralOrientationMeeting,ONB2MeetingTypeConfig,ONB2OffboardingMeetingTypeConfig,ONB2MessageActivity,ONB2ActivitiesConfig,ONB2ScheduledMessageActivity,ONB2MessageRecipient,ONB2OffboardingActivitiesConfig,ONB2UsefulContactActivity,ONB2UsefulContact,ONB2WhatToBringActivity,ONB2WhatToBringConfig,ONB2WhatToBringItemConfig,ONB2ChecklistActivity,ONB2ChecklistItem,ONB2AssetActivity,ONB2AssetActivityFieldControls,ONB2Asset,ONB2AssetCategoryConfig,ONB2EquipmentType,ONB2EquipmentTypeValue,ComplianceProcess,ComplianceProcessTask,ComplianceProcessResponsible,ComplianceProcessTaskType,MDFEnumValue,I9UserData,restartOnboardingProcess,getOnboardingDocuments,getBPEUserTaskUrl,getBPETodoUserTaskUrl,ONB2WhereToGoActivity,ONB2ActivityNudgeDetails,ONB2ProcessResponsible,ONB2ProcessTask,PickListValueV2 is not found. Please contact your system administrator.

entry.title: Python call a store procedure
entry.content:
I have statement to call a store procedure using python.... The store procedure executes an insert  statement .

but the call seems not to do anything.....

def execute_proc(self, sql_statement):
"""
Execute a Store Procedure.
"""
try:
cursor = self.connection.cursor()
cursor.execute("SET SCHEMA HISTDBA")
cursor.callproc(sql_statement)
self.connection.commit()
cursor.close()
print(f"Executed SQL: {sql_statement}")
except Exception as err:

This the code i used to call store procedure. I am able to call the procedure from studio. I also add it the select permission to the account used to execute the python code.
print(f"Error executing SQL: {err}")
raise

entry.title: Re: Deployment Error : 403 error in Fiori App deployed in...
entry.content:
Hi Arasu,

deploying the Fiori app to S/4HANA Public Cloud works only when the backend service that you call is coming from S/4HANA itself. In your case you need to deploy the Frontend to BTP. I would suggest you use the managed approuter (see https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/deployment/to-cf#sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition). 

Best regards
Gregor

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Lessons Learned: CSRF Token troubleshooting with S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Lucas,

have you checked CSRF-Token Handling in the CAP Documentation? Your custom code should not be required.

Best regards,
Gregor

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services for Beginners
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Cloud Identity services consist of 3 key components.

Identity Authentication (IAS): Manages user login and provides single sign-on.

Identity Provisioning (IPS): Syncs user data between systems. It does the transformation/filtering from source to target system. 

Identity Directory: Centralizes user information, acting as a single source of truth for identity management.

In this blog post, I will showcase the following:

  • Use IAS as a custom IDP for Integration Suite
  • Provision users from FSM to IAS using IPS

Configure IAS as custom IDP for BTP Subaccount using

OIDC

In this scenario, we have two subaccounts, say trial and cis. Subaccount named - trial has Integration Suite subscribed and Subaccount named - cis has Cloud Identity Services subscribed.

Step 1:

Go to the Sub Account of Integration Suite for trust configuration.

Click on ‘Establish Trust’ Button.

 

 

The login page of Integration Suite looks like below:

Step 2:

Go to Cloud Identity Services -> Users & Authorizations -> User Management -> Add New User

After account activation, the user (in our case Mr. John Doe) still cannot access the Integration Suite. See the screenshot below. This brings to the next step.

Step 3:

Go to Subaccount for Integration Suite.

Create a custom role collection containing all the required roles of SAP CPI Consultant.

Step 4:

Go to Cloud Identity Services.

Create a group for CPI consultants and assign the user to that group.

Step 5:

After establishing trust (referring to step 1), an application is auto-generated, as shown below.

Now there is an attribute related to ‘group’.

Go to subaccount for Integration Suite -> Trust Configuration -> Click on Custom IDP link 

Step 6:

When a user logins, we can see the logs from Troubleshooting log of Cloud Identity Service.

Monitoring & Reporting -> Troubleshooting Logs -> search for keyword ‘jwt’.

Action type is ‘issueJwtToken’.

Click on ‘Log Details’.

Step 7:

Enable 'Risk-based Authentication' for email domain.

Now if the user's email domain is gmail, it will ask for two-factor authentication.

Go to Profile Management UI.

Provision users from FSM to IAS using IPS

Step 1:

Create source system for FSM (Field Service Management)

For this case, I will replicate only one dummy user from FSM to IAS. The filter criteria (fsm.user.filter) is used.

Please note the data used in executing these scenarios throughout this blog post is test data/ fake data only.

Ignore 'Group' entity (to avoid group sync).

Step 2:

Create target system for IAS (Identity Authentication)

Create a system admin for IAS and use the generated client id as user and secret as password.

Step 3:

Run 'Read Job'. It is also possible to subscribe for job failure notifications by clicking on 'Subscribe'.

Step 4:

Go to Provisioning log to check the job execution status and details.

Reference Links:

  1. SAP Cloud Identity Services
  2. Field Service Management - SCIM API
  3. SAP IPS - List of All Properties available for User Sync

Regards,

Priyanka Chakraborti

entry.content:

When trying to close the phases in transaction QE51N, it displays the following error (SAP ECC):

Status in QA03: INSP RREC PRII

entry.title: Re: How to add and remove measure in table using sac script
entry.content: Hello all, not sure if this solves the problem, as was already mentioned the code is not pasted here but what helped me was that wherever there is ""Account_BestRunJ_sold" like for example here: Table.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Account_BestRunJ_sold",selectedIds); replace the "Account_BestRunJ_sold" with Alias.MeasureDimension (without any quotations)

entry.title: Disabling automatically generated pseduo comments or pragmas in SAP GUI custom objects / classes
entry.content:
Hi all!

In a previous project that I was involved, the pseudo comments or pragmas were NOT automatically generated on creation of a custom class in SAP GUI.

However in this new project I am involved, it does generate and add the pragmas/pseudo comments at the end of the code lines. This is something I do not want to deal with. I remember these pragmas or pseudo codes were controlled and only added, once the ATC results were in priority 1 or 2.

What is the way to do that change in this new project?  Any idea? Is there any snote for that or sth?

Note: I want to solve this issue on SAP GUI side not on Eclipse. Thanks in advance 🙂

entry.title: Enterprise portfolio and project management (EPPM)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

As an intermediate SAP consultant with a solid understanding of EPPM, I am expanding my knowledge to SAP Portfolio and Project Management (PPM) and Commercial Project Management (CPM). I’m eager to learn the business process steps and configuration steps for these modules to effectively implement and align them with organizational needs.

Areas I Need Help With:

1️⃣ Business Process Steps

  • How do PPM and CPM processes integrate with the broader project and portfolio lifecycle in a typical organization?
  • What are the critical business scenarios or use cases for PPM and CPM?
  • Any tips on understanding end-to-end workflows for project portfolio prioritization, financial planning, and profitability management?

2️⃣ Configuration Steps

  • What are the foundational configuration activities for setting up PPM and CPM?
  • How do I handle integration with other SAP modules like PS, FI/CO, MM, or SD?
  • Are there specific tools or methodologies within PPM and CPM that require extra attention during setup?

3️⃣ Best Practices and Challenges

  • What are the common pitfalls or challenges during configuration and implementation?
  • Are there any resources or documentation you recommend to overcome these challenges?

The token from xsuaaforhuman service instance just does not contain the scope scopeforproviderapp. Also it is unclear for me how Postman can know my user details when calling the /getData endpoint.

The first and third part of the blog worked fine for me.

entry.title: 12 Tips for Beginners Starting with SAP ABAP Programming
entry.content:
Welcome to Our SAP ABAP Programming Guide!

Are you starting your journey into the world of SAP ABAP programming? You've come to the right place! In this post, we’ve compiled 12 essential tips to help you navigate the initial steps of learning ABAP. Whether you're a complete beginner or have some basic knowledge, these tips will provide you with a solid foundation to build your skills.

12 Tips for Beginners Starting with SAP ABAP Programming

1. Understand the Basics of SAP
Before diving into ABAP, familiarize yourself with the fundamentals of SAP. Learn about SAP modules, the ERP system, and how different components interact.

2. Learn the Syntax and Structure
Start by understanding the basic syntax and structure of ABAP. Get comfortable with keywords, data types, and basic programming constructs.

3. Use the ABAP Development Tools (ADT)
Get accustomed to using ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse. It’s essential for efficient development and offers features like syntax highlighting, code completion, and debugging tools.

4. Understand Data Dictionary Objects
Learn how to work with Data Dictionary (DDIC) objects such as tables, views, data elements, and domains. These are crucial for database interactions in ABAP.

5. Practice Modularization Techniques
Master the use of subroutines, function modules, methods, and classes to write modular and reusable code. This will make your programs more maintainable and efficient.

6. Get Familiar with Internal Tables
Internal tables are a key feature in ABAP for handling data in-memory. Practice creating, populating, and manipulating internal tables.

7. Understand ALV Reports
Learn how to create and customize ALV (ABAP List Viewer) reports. ALV provides a flexible way to output data with features like sorting, filtering, and layout customization.

8. Explore Open SQL
SAP ABAP uses Open SQL for database operations. Learn the basics of SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements, and how to handle database transactions.

9. Practice Debugging
Get comfortable with the debugging tools in ABAP. Knowing how to effectively debug your code is crucial for identifying and fixing issues.

10. Learn About BAPIs and RFCs
BAPIs (Business Application Programming Interfaces) and RFCs (Remote Function Calls) are essential for integrating different SAP systems. Understand how to use them to enable communication between systems.

11. Get Hands-On with Enhancements and Exits
Learn about user exits, BADI (Business Add-Ins), and enhancement spots to customize and extend standard SAP functionality.

12. Engage with the SAP Community
Join the SAP community through forums, blogs, and SAP’s official website. Engaging with experienced professionals can provide valuable insights and help you stay updated with the latest developments.

entry.title: RE-FX Intereses
entry.content:
Buenos días Estimados,

Quisiera hacerles 2 preguntas a ver si alguien puede ayudarme:

1) He cargado un contrato en RECN, cuyo flujo de caja coincide casi entero con el cronograma provisto por el cliente, pero tengo una pequeña diferencia en los intereses, el cual no logro encontrar la manera de ajustarlos. 

En donde se completa la tasa de interes, completo con la proporcionada por el banco para el leasing, y el desglose de intereses a lo largo del contrato, no coincide...saben si hay alguna manera de ajustar los intereses de manera manual?

 

2) Por otra parte, el contrato en cuestión, lleva un año ya devengado, pero el modulo de REFX recien ahora en diciembre se implemento. Lo transcurrido durante el 2024, fue contabilizado desde el modulo FI, cual es la mejor practica para realizar este ajuste y continuar llevandolo por medio del modulo REFX sin duplicar movimientos?

 

Desde ya les re agradezco el tiempo.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP Error Message not displaying
entry.content: @AbhishekSharma it is in save_modified and failed parameter is not available in it.

entry.title: Dual control sensitive fields for vendor master in sap
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

We have a requirement for vendor master data changes that need to go through an approval process. To facilitate this, we have activated the dual control sensitive fields for the vendor master.

Currently, when changes are made to the vendor master, the system prompts for confirmation of the sensitive fields by another user. However, the system is still allowing PO creation even if the changes in the vendor master fields have not been approved through TCode FK08.

Is there a solution to ensure that POs cannot be created for vendors with unapproved master data changes?

Looking forward to your guidance. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.@sandeep_kumar

 

Regards, 

Mohammed Nafees

entry.title: Re: How do you share a postgresql instance across multipl...
entry.content: I think the problem is not with @cap-js/postgres because i have checked the code and it is working fine, i.e. if i pass the schema variable in cds.requires.database parameter locally it is picking the right schema (It is assuming that the schema is already created though). The problem is with the binding. If there was any way the cf broker can accept a schema when creating a binding or when creating a serviceKey, this would solve the problem

SS

Can you please refer to the below SAP Help Documentation for the same
Customer and Internal Project Management
Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

Scope item for customer Project Management - J11
Scope item for internal Project Management -  1A8
Scope item for Project Control-Finance - 1NT
Scope item for Project Review - 1YF

All the Scope item for Project control

entry.title: Re: Deployment Error : 403 error in Fiori App deployed in...

Thank you for your response and the helpful insights!

  1. Are there any options available to push the Fiori app to S/4HANA Public Cloud if I deploy the application in BTP HTML5 Apps if so what are the service i need in BTP?
  2.  Is there any option available to use the CAP service backend while creating a proxy-like setup in S/4HANA Public Cloud, so it is treated as the cloud's own backend, even though the actual backend remains in CAP?

Looking forward to your guidance!

Best regards,
Arasu

entry.title: HANA Sizing Keypoints
entry.content:
SAP HANA sizing is a crucial step in preparing your system for both current business needs and future growth. Whether you’re planning for Suite on SAP HANA or transitioning to S/4HANA, getting the sizing right ensures optimal performance, cost-efficiency, and scalability. This blog breaks down key aspects of SAP HANA sizing, complete with easy-to-follow formulas and examples.

1. Data Volume Sizing: Planning for Growth

Data sizing is the backbone of your SAP HANA setup. It ensures there’s enough space for your data today and accounts for growth in the years to come. SAP recommends the following formula:

Key Factors:

  • Net Data Volume on Disk: The current size of your data on disk. For example, this might be 149.7 GiB based on your analysis.
  • Factor 1.2: Adds a 20% buffer for operations like Delta Merge.
  • Year-Over-Year (YoY) Growth Factor: Accounts for expected growth. For instance, 15% annual growth over three years gives a factor of 1.52=(1.15)31.52 = (1.15)^31.52=(1.15)3.

This formula not only ensures there’s enough space but also prepares your system for seamless future expansions.

2. Log Volume Sizing: Securing Data Integrity

Log volumes are critical for maintaining database integrity during savepoints and backups. The size of the log volume depends on the amount of RAM and system workload. Use the following formula:

 

​

 

Examples:

  • For a system with 128 GB RAM, log volume = 64 GB.
  • For a system with 512 GB RAM, log volume = 256 GB.
  • For a system with 1 TB RAM or more, the minimum recommended log volume is 512 GB.

It’s essential to account for higher workloads or delays in savepoints, which might require additional log volume space to prevent issues.

3. Installation Disk Space: Keeping It Simple

When planning your disk space for SAP HANA installation, you should allocate enough space for /hana/shared/<SID>. For single-node installations, the formula is straightforward:

Examples:

  • A system with 128 GB RAM → Installation Size = 128 GB.
  • A system with 1 TB RAM → Installation Size = 1 TB.
  • A system with 2 TB RAM → Installation Size = 1 TB (maximum limit for single-node).

For scale-out systems, calculate the installation size based on the combined requirements of all nodes.

4. Disk Space for Backups: Ensuring Safety

Backups are your safety net. You need sufficient space to store both full and delta backups. Here’s the formula:

​

Backup Cycle:

  • For a typical 28-day backup cycle:
    • Weekends: Full backups.
    • Workdays: Delta backups.

Having adequate backup space ensures smooth operations, even during emergencies or system failures.

5. Export Disk Space: Preparing for Troubleshooting

In some cases, like root cause analysis, you might need to export database content. SAP recommends reserving space equivalent to twice the size of the largest table in your database:

This ensures that even in critical situations, you have the resources to perform a detailed analysis.

6. Network Requirements: Keeping It Fast and Reliable

For SAP HANA, your network plays a critical role in ensuring fast communication between components. Here are the minimum bandwidth requirements:

  • Internal and Storage Zones: ≥ 10 Gb Ethernet (GbE) or ≥ 8 Gb Fibre Channel (GbF).
  • Proper segmentation of networks is also recommended, separating client, internal, storage, and admin zones for better performance and security.

7. CPU Sizing: Planning for User Load

SAP HANA CPU sizing depends on the number of active users and their workload. Use this formula for a rough estimate:

​

For example, a system with 200 active users would need approximately:

This estimate ensures your system can handle user workloads without exceeding 65% CPU utilization.

Conclusion

Sizing SAP HANA isn’t just about crunching numbers—it’s about future-proofing your system for growth and ensuring seamless performance. By following these formulas and best practices, you can confidently size your SAP HANA setup for today’s demands and tomorrow’s challenges.

References

  1. SAP HANA Master Guide
    SAP HANA Master Guide

  2. SAP Notes

    • SAP Note 1872170: ABAP on HANA Sizing Report
    • SAP Note 2407186: High Availability Guides.
    • SAP Note 1900823: SAP HANA Storage Connector API.
    • SAP Note 2786237: Persistent Memory Sizing.
    • SAP Note 1793345: Sizing for SAP Suite on SAP HANA.
    • SAP Note 1637145: SAP BW on HANA Sizing.

  3. SAP White Papers

    • SAP HANA Network Requirements

By keeping these guidelines in mind, you can set up your SAP HANA system to not just function but thrive.


Source:https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/installing-and-administering-sap-hana/performing-sap-hana-sizing

Liked it? Don't forget to give a kudos! or atleast feedback, will improvise

Happy Learning

#HANA, #/SDF/HDB_SIZING

entry.title: Re: How can get the two last record of a internal table a...
entry.content:
Hello @PTecnico ,

In terms of performance, Approach 2 is the most efficient method to fetch the last two records from an internal table. Based on the current capabilities of SAP ABAP and HANA, these are the primary approaches available for this use case. As of now, there are no additional or more advanced syntaxes specifically designed to directly fetch the last two records from an internal table.

Thanks & regards,

Satya.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Architecture Guide
entry.content: @HammadSharif1 , great thought process for diving into integration solution designing. Kudos

entry.title: Re: Versioning in Analytic Data Model (SAC)
entry.content:
@Artur3 

I am not sure at what stage you got the error but Let me clarify few things.

You can have multiple versions Like Actual, Plan, Budget, Forecast in Analytic Model and also load the data to it. The restriction is that you cannot have more than one version with version category set as Actuals as shown below

But As you can see I have a version called PL with category set as Planning and you can load data to this version without any issues.

While mapping the file just assign the version PL or any version which you have in your model as shown below.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Return the Stock in WM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are doing the Initial stock entry with 561 Mvt type with reference of TR number Putaway is done to the destination bin ( 123-storage type) and further we are doing the internal transfer from 123 to 456 storage type.

Requirement is customer wants to return the stock from 456 storage type.
Kindly suggest the Solution and supporting the BAPI for the solution.

Thanks,
Saikumar

entry.title: Re: Max Value highlight with Cross Calculation and Calcul...
entry.content:
@AkshaySakhpara 

Haven't tried this on a DS Analytic model but you could see if the steps in the below blog works or not.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/how-to-highlight-max-largest-value-of-a-measure-or-account-in-a-row-or/ba-p/13531932

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Could not create service "Cloud-Logging" : Service plan Lite Not Found
entry.content:
Hello Geeks,

The below things I am trying to do it in my BTP Trial A/C

I am trying to implement "Observability" in my CAPM application. When I try to deploy the application in the cloud foundry I am getting the following error - 

 

Service operation failed: Controller operation failed: 404 Not Found: Error creating or updating service instance: Could not create service "Test-cloud-logging" : Service plan lite not found.

 

The video which i referred is this - Implement Observability in a Full-Stack CAP Application Following SAP BTP Developer’s Guide 

Package.JSON

 

"cds": {
    "requires": {
      "db": "hana",
      "auth": "mocked",
      "connectivity": true,
      "destinations": true,
      "html5-repo": true,
      "cloud-logging": true,
      "[production]": {
        "telemetry": {
          "kind": "to-cloud-logging"
        }
      }
    },
    "sql": {
      "native_hana_associations": false
    }
  }

 

MTA.YAML

 

- name: Test-cloud-logging
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: cloud-logging
      service-plan: lite
      config:
        ingest_otlp:
          enabled: true
- name: Test-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      readiness-health-check-type: http
      readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm
    provides:
      - name: srv-api # required by consumers of CAP services (e.g. approuter)
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    requires:
      - name: Test-auth
      - name: Test-db
      - name: Test-connectivity
      - name: Test-destination
      - name: Test-cloud-logging

entry.title: Re: SMS text notifications are useless
entry.content: The SAP product is Sales Performance Management under SuccessFactors umbrella. The notification is coming via SMS text messaging system.

entry.title: Future of Talent Profile and Growth Portfolio
entry.content:
Hi SAPians, 
I need your expertise on the future of the Talent Profile and Growth Portfolio in SuccessFactors. My company is considering whether to keep the Talent Profile or transition to the Growth Portfolio. Specifically, I have the following questions:

  • What is the future of the Talent Profile in SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite, and what is SAP's vision for it moving forward? Is it being phased out in favor of the Growth Portfolio
  • If there is a migration from the Talent Profile to the Growth Portfolio, which blocks could be migrated?
  • Does SAP have any plans to integrate the Talent Profile into the Growth Portfolio

Thank you for your insights!

entry.title: Re: Unable to activate Account for Identity Authentication Service via email link
entry.content: Are you saying you took a new trial account, ran the booster for Build Apps, which then created Cloud Identity Service and sent you an email, but the link in the email does not work?

entry.title: Re: Unable to activate Account for Identity Authenticatio...
entry.content: If you open the Cloud Identity Service instance, you should be taken to the same place. Does this work for you?

entry.title: Re: Automatic Supplier Disqualification based on Certificate Expiry in SLP
entry.content:
Dear Velu,

We do have similar requirement to block/ deactivate supplier based on Certificate expiry. Could you please suggest if you manage to implement this.

entry.title: Re: Could not create service "Cloud-Logging" : Service pl...
entry.content:
The service plan that I can see in theEntitlements of my Free Tier SAP Partner subaccount  is called dev:

According to the SAP Discovery center entry for SAP Cloud Logging there is no lite plan. I've also checked if I could assign Cloud Logging in my BTP Trial. But it's not even there:

I hope that someone responsible for Cloud Logging from SAP will help to close this gap.

entry.title: Re: Could not create service "Cloud-Logging" : Service pl...
entry.content: Maybe @Hariharan-Gandhi can help here?

entry.title: Re: Deployment Error : 403 error in Fiori App deployed in...
entry.content:
Hi Arasu,

I would suggest you check out the blog post Enterprise Resource Planning Blogs by SAP. There especially the "Decision matrix – When to use which extensibility option?". If your target group are Employees who use S/4HANA all day long is I see this options:

  1. Use a Custom Tile to point to the BTP Frontend
  2. Publish S/4HANA to SAP Build Work Zone (Content Providers - Cloud Solutions) and integrate the BTP Frontend there as an additional app
  3. Develop your extenstion on stack using Embedded Steampunk

Best regards,
Gregor

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements Developer Extensibility Doubt
Re: How to Connect SAP Build Apps to Demo S/4HANA Data
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski ,

Wish you a very hapy new year. Can I connect a custom API with build apps? The custom API is created in my S4PC. In that case, what would be my API keys?
